items.title: Caspian Pipeline Consortium Halts Loadings After Drone Strike
items.description: Oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium’s terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast was halted after a drone strike.

items.title: US, Iran Launch Fresh Strikes After Two Troops Die in Jordan
items.description: The US and Iran engaged in another wave of tit-for-tat attacks, signaling no let-up after strikes by the Islamic Republic killed two American service members in Jordan on Friday.

items.title: Jingye Says It Reserves Legal Rights in UK Steel Nationalization
items.description: Jingye Steel Co. has reserved all legal rights, including international arbitration, over the UK government’s nationalization of British Steel.

items.title: South Korea Details Plan for Foreigners to Trade Won Easier
items.description: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the forex market and bring the currency closer to full convertibility.

items.title: Venezuela’s Official Earthquake Death Toll Rises to 5,119
items.description: The official death toll from the twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela last month rose to 5,119, according to National Assembly head Jorge Rodríguez.

items.title: Korea’s AI-Heavy Market Now Sets the Tone for Global Stocks
items.description: For fund managers in London, New York and Tokyo, a new ritual has emerged before trading gets underway: checking South Korean stocks.

items.title: Vanishing CLO Profits Are Sparking Infighting: Credit Weekly
items.description: A type of investment that once generated some of Wall Street’s juiciest fixed-income returns has deteriorated so badly that investors are heading for the exits and arguing about who’s to blame.

items.title: Kuwait Energy Infrastructure Hit During Intense Iran Attacks
items.description: Kuwait suffered one of its worst nights of Iranian retaliatory attacks since the Middle East conflict began, with strikes on an oil facility and a second power plant hit in as many days.
